criteria.label: Read conflict markers (with or without `diff3` style) and identify each side's intent.
criteria.example: `<<<<<<< HEAD` = my side. `=======` = divider. `>>>>>>> branch` = their side. With diff3, the `||||||| ancestor` block shows the baseline.

criteria.label: Follow the resolve → `git add` → continue/commit workflow without consulting docs.
criteria.example: After editing: `git status` (confirm no 'both modified') → `git add <files>` → `git rebase --continue` (or `git commit`).

criteria.label: Configure `merge.conflictStyle = diff3` and use `git mergetool` with your preferred 3-way differ.
criteria.example: `git config --global merge.conflictStyle diff3` plus a VS Code or kdiff3 mergetool setup.

criteria.label: Decide between continuing and aborting based on conflict scale and rebase vs merge tradeoff.
criteria.example: 20-conflict rebase: `git rebase --abort` and `git merge origin/main` instead — one resolution surface.

criteria.label: Use specialized tooling for notebooks and binaries (`nbdime`, `nbstripout`, Git LFS).
criteria.example: `nbdime config-git --enable --global` so notebook diffs/merges are semantic, not JSON.

criteria.label: Detect and recover from semantic conflicts that Git auto-merged but tests catch.
criteria.example: Auto-merge succeeded but `pytest` fails: trace which two changes interact, fix the integration, add a regression test.
